title.purpose: Claim groups, evidence sections, and inspector headings.

label.purpose: Status, filters, and compact table headings.

mono.purpose: Commands, paths, hashes, timestamps, and raw evidence.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Menus, command palettes, and tooltips that physically overlap evidence.

shadows.name: sticky-edge
shadows.purpose: Separates sticky summary regions from scrolling evidence.

motion.name: ease-investigate
motion.purpose: Short state changes and evidence disclosure.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Commits a high-confidence action such as exporting or verifying a bundle.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Navigates or copies evidence without competing with the primary action.

components.name: Evidence Search
components.description: Filters claims, findings, files, commands, and raw evidence in place.

components.name: Evidence Navigation
components.description: Keeps the verdict-to-raw-evidence path explicit and keyboard navigable.
components.html: Overview 1Claims 4Evidence 8
components.css: .ds-nav{display:grid;gap:4px;width:220px;font:600 0.875rem/1.3 Inter,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-nav a{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;min-height:40px;padding:8px 10px;border-radius:7px;color:oklch(0.455 0.030 235);text-decoration:none;transition:background 120ms,color 120ms}.ds-nav a:hover{background:oklch(0.965 0.008 230);color:oklch(0.190 0.025 235)}.ds-nav a.active{background:oklch(0.925 0.014 230);color:oklch(0.410 0.125 230)}.ds-nav a:focus-visible{outline:2px solid oklch(0.570 0.160 230);outline-offset:2px}.ds-nav span{font:650 .75rem/1.25 ui-monospace,monospace}

components.name: Proven Status
components.description: An earned semantic state containing an icon and explicit status text.

components.name: Claim Evidence
components.description: A compact claim row that exposes state, explanation, and supporting evidence count.
components.html:
CLAIM · BEHAVIOR

Requested behavior is covered by tests.

✓ Proven

Test command and test-integrity rule passed.

3 evidence records
components.css: .ds-claim{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr auto;gap:8px 16px;max-width:620px;background:white;color:oklch(0.190 0.025 235);border:1px solid oklch(0.850 0.014 230);border-radius:12px;padding:16px 18px;font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-claim h3{margin:3px 0 0;font-size:1.05rem;line-height:1.3}.ds-claim p{grid-column:1/-1;margin:4px 0;color:oklch(0.455 0.030 235);font-size:.875rem}.ds-claim code{grid-column:1/-1;color:oklch(0.410 0.125 230);font:450 .75rem/1.4 ui-monospace,monospace}.ds-kicker{color:oklch(0.455 0.030 235);font:650 .7rem/1.2 ui-monospace,monospace;letter-spacing:.04em}.ds-state{align-self:start;background:oklch(0.520 0.135 150);color:white;border-radius:999px;padding:5px 9px;font-size:.75rem;font-weight:650}.ds-claim:hover{border-color:oklch(0.570 0.160 230)}.ds-claim:focus-within{outline:2px solid oklch(0.570 0.160 230);outline-offset:2px}

narrative.overview:
PatchProof feels like a precise evidence table in a bright engineering lab. The canvas is neutral and calm; cobalt marks active reasoning, orange marks annotations, and semantic colors appear only where a verifier has produced an actual state. The interface is compact enough for experts while preserving a clear path from verdict to raw evidence.

The visual system rejects generic dark AI dashboards, decorative terminal chrome, and opaque trust meters. Delight comes from the proof graph resolving, a counterexample appearing beside a claim, and a hash chain visibly closing. Familiar controls keep the investigator focused on the evidence.
keyCharacteristics: Light, neutral working canvas with crisp cobalt navigation.
Dense evidence arranged through hierarchy, not nested cards.
Monospaced detail only where data provenance benefits from it.
Semantic status always paired with an icon and explicit text.
Short, state-driven transitions with full reduced-motion support.

rules.name: The Earned Color Rule
rules.body: Semantic color appears only when a verifier or user action has produced a state.

rules.name: The One Active Blue Rule
rules.body: Proof Blue identifies the current path or action. It never competes with status colors.

rules.name: The Provenance Type Rule
rules.body: Monospace means the value can be copied, compared, or verified. It is never decorative.

rules.name: The Flat Evidence Rule
rules.body: Evidence is separated by alignment and rules. A shadow must communicate actual overlap.

dos: Do connect every verdict, claim, and finding to inspectable evidence.
Do use Proof Blue for the current path and reserve semantic colors for real verifier states.
Do keep logs monospaced, selectable, line-wrapped by default, and expandable for full output.
Do preserve familiar controls, visible focus, 40px minimum targets, and reduced-motion behavior.
Do show uncertainty in plain language, including what was not executed or could not be proven.
donts: Don't create generic dark AI dashboards with purple gradients, neon glows, or decorative terminal chrome.
Don't present conversational summaries as evidence or hide raw verifier output.
Don't use equal-card SaaS grids, fake activity metrics, or abstract trust scores with no inspectable inputs.
Don't use security fear, opaque severity, or red everywhere instead of explaining the exact source-to-result chain.
Don't turn the report into an agent launcher or worktree dashboard.
Don't use colored side-stripe borders, gradient text, glassmorphism, or wide decorative shadows.
